content: Which prophet confronted David about his sin with Bathsheba?
explanation: 2 Samuel 12:7 - The prophet Nathan used the parable of the little lamb to lead David to condemn himself, then declared: "You are the man!"

content: How many years in total did David reign as king?
explanation: 2 Samuel 5:4-5 - David became king at age 30 and reigned for 40 years.
options: 40 years (7 years in Hebron, 33 years in Jerusalem)

content: What did David do when the Ark of the Covenant was brought into Jerusalem?
explanation: 2 Samuel 6:14 - David danced before the Lord with all his might, without worrying about his kingly image. Wholehearted worship.
options: Danced before the Lord with all his might

content: What important promise did the Davidic Covenant (2 Samuel 7) contain?
explanation: 2 Samuel 7:12-13 - The everlasting throne of David's lineage is fulfilled in Jesus Christ (Luke 1:32-33).
options: The temple would never be destroyed
The throne of David's lineage would endure forever

content: To whom did David show kindness because of his promise to Jonathan?
explanation: 2 Samuel 9:7 - Mephibosheth was lame in both feet but was invited by David to eat at the king's table for the rest of his life.

content: When did David's great sin with Bathsheba begin?
explanation: 2 Samuel 11:1-2 - In the season when kings went out to battle, David stayed home. Idleness led to temptation and sin.
options: When David remained in Jerusalem instead of going to battle

content: What did David do to cover up his sin with Bathsheba?
explanation: 2 Samuel 11:15 - David wrote a letter to Joab to place Uriah in the most dangerous position. One sin led to another - indirect murder.
options: Arranged for her husband Uriah to die in battle

content: When his son died, what did David say about his hope?
explanation: 2 Samuel 12:23 - David believed he would meet his son in the afterlife. Hope of life after death.
options: I shall go to him, but he shall not return to me

content: Which of David's sons rebelled and proclaimed himself king?
explanation: 2 Samuel 15:13-14 - Absalom rebelled, and David had to flee from Jerusalem. A consequence of family sin.

content: How did David react when Absalom died?
explanation: 2 Samuel 18:33 - Although Absalom had rebelled, David was still a grieving father. "O Absalom, my son, my son!"
options: Rejoiced that the rebellion was over
Wept and wished he had died in his son's place

content: David's last words emphasized what about God's covenant?
explanation: 2 Samuel 23:5 - David affirmed the everlasting covenant that God had made with his house.

content: What did David say when buying the threshing floor to build an altar?
explanation: 2 Samuel 24:24 - True sacrifice requires cost. He would not offer to God something that cost him nothing.
options: I will not offer to the Lord sacrifices that cost me nothing
I will pay the best price
